name: markdown-resume-suite
description: Write your resume in Markdown, and style it with CSS (we have templates). Export it as a PDF that actually looks good.

commands.title: Markdown Resume Suite: Export Resume | PDF

markdown-resume-suite.inputMarkdownDirec.markdownDescription:
The path of the directory where your resume's Markdown file lives. 

 If empty, defaults to the directory of the currently-open file when a relevant command is called.

markdown-resume-suite.inputMarkdownFilen.markdownDescription: The filename of your resume's Markdown file (with the `.md` extension).

markdown-resume-suite.inputStyleSheetDir.markdownDescription:
The path of the directory where your resume's CSS style sheet lives. 

 If empty, defaults to the directory of the currently-open file when a relevant command is called.

markdown-resume-suite.inputStyleSheetFil.markdownDescription: The filename of your resume's CSS style sheet (with the `.css` extension).

markdown-resume-suite.outputDirectory.markdownDescription:
The path of the directory where you wish your exported resume to be created. 

 If empty, defaults to the directory of the currently-open file when a relevant command is called.

markdown-resume-suite.outputFilename.markdownDescription: The filename under which you wish your exported resume to be created (without any given extension).

markdown-resume-suite.templateDirectory.markdownDescription:
The path of the directory where resume templates will be created.

Templates are delivered as folders containing a Markdown file, a CSS style sheet and any other files/folders used to create the final document (e.g., image files). 

 If empty, defaults to the directory of the currently-open file when a relevant command is called.

scripts.pretest: yarn run compile && yarn run lint
